description: A comfort dish made from macaroni combined with a cheese sauce or layered with cheese and baked. It may be creamy on the stovetop or baked with a browned topping.
preparation_steps: Cook the macaroni until just tender.
Prepare a cheese sauce, often starting with butter, flour, and milk.
Stir in cheese until melted and smooth.
Combine the pasta with the sauce.
Serve as is or bake with extra cheese or breadcrumbs on top.

health_advice.benefits: Provides energy and can supply calcium and protein from cheese and milk.
health_advice.risks: Often high in calories, saturated fat, and sodium, especially in rich baked versions.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Balance the portion size and pair it with vegetables or a salad.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional versions are vegetarian but not vegan. Vegan and gluten-free versions are common when the pasta and sauce are adjusted.

allergens: Gluten unless gluten-free pasta is used
